sections.title: Talking to him again before meeting Osman

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.text: Have you found the spymaster, Osman, yet? You cannot proceed in your task without reporting to him.

sections.title: Talking to him after meeting Osman

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.text: I understand the Spymaster has hired you. I will pay the reward only when the Prince is rescued.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.text: Greetings I am Hassan, Chancellor to the Emir of Al- Kharid.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.text: I need the services of someone, yes. If you are interested, see the spymaster, Osman. I manage the finances here. Come to me when you need payment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me what I still need to get?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: A print of the key in soft clay and a bronze bar. Then, collect the key from Leela.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: You can collect the key from Leela.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: The key you already have. Good.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: You need to make a blonde wig somehow. Leela may help.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: The wig you have got; well done.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: You will need a skirt that looks the same as Keli's.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: You have the skirt. Good.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Something to make the prince's skin appear lighter.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: You have the skin paint; well done. I thought you would struggle to make that.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Yes, you have the rope.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: A rope with which to tie Keli up.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Once you have everything, go to Leela. She must be ready to get the prince away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The chancellor trusts me. I have come for instructions.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Our prince is captive by the Lady Keli. We just need to make the rescue. There are two things we need you to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is the first thing I must do?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: The prince is guarded by some stupid guards and a clever woman. The woman is our only way to get the prince out. Only she can walk freely about the area.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: I think you will need to tie her up. One coil of rope should do for that. Then, disguise the prince as her to get him out without suspicion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How good must the disguise be?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Only enough to fool the guards at a distance. Get a skirt like hers. Same colour, same style. We will only have a short time.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Get a blonde wig, too. That is up to you to make or find. Something to colour the skin of the prince.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: My daughter and top spy, Leela, can help you. She has sent word that she has discovered where they are keeping the prince.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: It's near Draynor Village. She is lurking somewhere near there now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is the second thing you need?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: We need the key, or we need a copy made. If you can get some soft clay then you can copy the key...

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: ...If you can convince Lady Keli to show it to you for a moment. She is very boastful. It should not be too hard.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Bring the imprint to me, with a bar of bronze.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I had better go find some things.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: May good luck travel with you. Don't forget to find Leela. It can't be done without her help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am here to help you free the prince.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Your employment is known to me. Now, do you know all that we need to make the break?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must make a disguise. What do you suggest?

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Only the lady Keli, can wander about outside the jail. The guards will shoot to kill if they see the prince out so we need a disguise good enough to fool them at a distance.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You need a wig, maybe made from wool. If you find someone who can work with wool ask them about it. There's a witch nearby may be able to help you dye it.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: The wig you have got, well done.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You will need to get a pink skirt, same as Keli's.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You have got the skirt, good.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: We still need something to colour the Prince's skin lighter. There's a witch close to here. She knows about many things. She may know some way to make the skin lighter.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You have the skin paint, well done. I thought you would struggle to make that.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You will still need some rope to tie up Keli, of course. I heard that there's a good rope maker around here.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You have rope I see, to tie up Keli. That will be the most dangerous part of the plan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get the key made.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Yes, that is most important. There is no way you can get the real key. It is on a chain around Keli's neck. Almost impossible to steal.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Get some soft clay and get her to show you the key somehow. Then take the print, with bronze, to my father.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do with the guards?

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Most of the guards will be easy. The disguise will get past them. The only guard who will be a problem will be the one at the door.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: We can discuss this more when you have the rest of the escape kit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will go and get the rest of the escape equipment.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Good, I shall await your return with everything.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Why, hello there, [lad/lass]. Me friends call me Ned. I was a man of the sea, but it's past me now. Could I be making or selling you some rope?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you make other things apart from rope?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: I'm sure I can. What are you thinking of?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you knit me a sweater?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Do I look like a member of a sewing circle? Be off wi' you. I have fought monsters that would turn your hair blue.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: I don't need to be laughed at just 'cos I'm getting a bit old.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about some sort of wig?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Well... That's an interesting thought. Yes, I think I could do something. Give me 3 balls of wool and I might be able to do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, I will get some. I think a wig would be useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have that now. Please, make me a wig.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Okay, I will have a go.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Here you go. How's that for a quick effort? Not bad I think!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Ned. There's more to you than meets the eye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will come back when I need you to make me one.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Well, it sounds like a challenge. Come to me if you need one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you repair the arrow holes in the back of my shirt?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Ah yes, it's a tough world these days. There's a few brave enough to attack from ten metres away.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: There you go, good as new.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Ned. Maybe next time they will attack me face to face.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't need anything.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: What can I help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you think of a way to make skin paste?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Why, it's one of my most popular potions. The women here, they like to have smooth looking skin. And I must admit, some of the men buy it as well.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: I can make it for you, just get me what's needed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need to make it?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Well dearie, you need a base for the paste. That's a mix of ash, flour and water. Then you need redberries to colour it as you want. Bring me those four items and I will make you some.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Yes I can, I see you already have the ingredients. Would you like me to mix some for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please. Mix me some skin paste.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: That should be simple. Hand the things to Aggie then.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Tourniquet, Fenderbaum, Tottenham, Marshmallow, MarbleArch.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: There you go dearie, your skin potion. That will make you look good at the Varrock dances.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you, I don't need any skin paste right now.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Okay dearie, that's always your choice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you the famous Lady Keli? Leader of the toughest gang of mercenary killers around?

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: I am Keli, you have heard of me then?

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: That's very kind of you to say. Reputations are not easily earned. I have managed to succeed where many fail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think Katrine is still tougher.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Well you can think that all you like. I know those blackarm cowards dare not leave the city. Out here, I am toughest. You can tell them that! Now get out of my sight, before I call my guards.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: There's always someone ready to spread rumours. I hear all sort of ridiculous things these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, you need not go into specific details.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Well, I can tell you I have a valuable prisoner here in my cells. I can expect a high reward to be paid very soon for this guy. I can't tell you who he is, but he is a lot colder now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must have been very skillful.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Yes, I did most of the work. We had to grab the Pr...

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Er, we had to grab him without his ten bodyguards noticing. It was a stroke of genius.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure they will pay?

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: They will pay, or we will cut his hair off and send it to them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about trying something tougher? Maybe cut his finger off?

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: That's a good idea, I could use talented people like you. I may call on you if I need work doing.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: There is no way to release him. The only key to the door is on a chain around my neck and the locksmith who made the lock died suddenly when he had finished.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: There is not another key like this in the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I see the key please? Just for a moment. It would be something I can tell my grandchildren. When you are even more famous than you are now.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: As you put it that way I am sure you can see it. You cannot steal the key, it is on a Runite chain.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Only for a moment then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you so much, you are too kind, o great Keli.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: You are welcome, run along now, I am very busy.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: It is the best way I know. Dead men tell no tales.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am glad I know none of your secrets, Keli.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: You must be new to this land then. EVERYONE knows of Lady Keli and her prowess with the sword.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: I have used a sword since I was a small girl. I stabbed three people before I was 6 years old.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: I need to do a lot of work, goodbye. When you get a little tougher, maybe I will give you a job.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: Well, you know of me now. I will wring your neck if you do not show respect.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do not show respect to killers and hoodlums.

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: You should, you really should. I am wealthy enough to place a bounty on your head. Or just remove your head myself. Now go, I am busy, too busy to fight a would-be hoodlum.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Hi, I'm Joe, door guard for Lady Keli.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, who are you guarding here?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Can't say, all very secret. You should get out of here. I am not supposed to talk while I guard.

sections.title: Talking to Osman after losing the key

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I lost that key you gave me.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Foolish [man/woman]! A new key will cost 15gp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, I have 15gp

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: That is good. I will have another key made. You may collect it from Leela.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't got 15 coins with me.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Then come back to me when you do.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Well done; we can make the key now.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Pick the key up from Leela.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I will try to find the other items.

sections.title: Talking to Leela after having all the required items
Talking to Leela again if the player lost the key

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I lost that key you gave me.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Foolish [man/woman]! A new key will cost 15 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't got 15 coins with me.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Then come back to me when you do.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: My father sent this key for you. Be careful not to lose it.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Good, you have all the basic equipment. Next to deal with the guard on the door. He is talkative, try to find a weakness in him.

sections.title: Talking to Joe once he is drunk

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Halt! Who goes there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello friend, I am just rescuing the prince, is that ok?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Thatsh a funny joke. You are lucky I am shober. Go in peace, friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some beer here, fancy one?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Ah, that would be lovely, just one now, just to wet my throat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, it must be tough being here without a drink.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: That was perfect, I can't thank you enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you? Still ok? Not too drunk?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: No, I don't get drunk with only one drink. You would need a few to do that. But thanks for the beer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you care for another beer, my friend?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: I better not, I don't want to be drunk on duty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, just keep these for later, I hate to see a thirsty guard.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Franksh, that wash just what I need to shtay on guard. No more beersh, I don't want to get drunk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the life of a guard.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Well, the hours are good.....

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: .... But most of those hours are a drag. If only I had spent more time in Knight school when I was a young boy. Maybe I wouldn't be here now, scared of Keli.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, chill out, I won't cause you trouble. I was just wondering what you do to relax.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: You never relax with these people, but it's a good career for a young man and some of the shouting I rather like.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: RESISTANCE IS USELESS!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you buy with these great wages?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Really, after working here, there's only time for a drink or three. All us guards go to the same bar and drink ourselves stupid.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: It's what I enjoy these days, that fade into unconciousness. I can't resist the sight of a really cold beer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want to be when you were a boy?

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Well, I loved to sit by the lake, with my toes in the water and shoot the fish with my bow and arrow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was a strange hobby for a little boy.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: It kept us from goblin hunting, which was what most boys did. What are you here for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, chill out, I won't cause you trouble. I was just wondering what you do to relax.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: You never relax with these people, but it's a good career for a young man and some of the shouting I rather like.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: RESISTANCE IS USELESS!

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: WHAT?! Are you trying to bribe me? I may not be a great guard, but I am loyal. How DARE you try to bribe me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, no, you got the wrong idea, totally. I just wondered if you wanted some part-time bodyguard work.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Oh. Sorry. No, I don't need money. As long as you were not offering me a bribe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had better leave, I don't want trouble.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Thanks, I appreciate that. Talking on duty can be punishable by having your mouth stitched up. These are tough people, no mistake.

sections.title: Talking to Leela again after speaking to Joe

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Ok now, you have all the basic equipment. What are your plans to stop the guard interfering?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't spoken to him yet.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Well, speaking to him may find a weakness he has. See if there's something that could stop him bothering us.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Good luck with the guard. When the guard is out you can tie up Keli.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was going to attack him.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: I don't think you should. If you do the rest of the gang and Keli would attack you. The door guard should be removed first, to make it easy.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Good luck with the guard. When the guard is out you can tie up Keli.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hoped to get him drunk.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Well, that's possible. These guards do like a drink. I would think it will take at least 3 beers to do it well. You would probably have to do it all at the same time too. The effects of the local beer wear off quickly.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Good luck with the guard. When the guard is out you can tie up Keli.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I could bribe him to leave.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You could try. I don't think the emir will pay anything towards it. And we did bribe one of their guards once.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Keli killed him in front of the other guards, as a deterrent. It would probably take a lot of gold.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Good luck with the guard. When the guard is out you can tie up Keli.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure yet.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You should try talking to him. He might give away some sort of weakness.

sections.title: Talking to Leela once Joe is drunk

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: You're back. How are things going with that guard?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's been dealt with.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Great! I think that means we're ready. Go in and use some rope to tie Keli up. Once she's dealt with, use the key to free the Prince. Don't forget to give him his disguise so the guards outside don't spot him.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Ali after he reappears

lines.speaker: Prince Ali
lines.text: I owe you my life for that escape. You cannot help me this time, they know who you are. Go in peace, friend of Al-Kharid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Prince, I come to rescue you.

lines.speaker: Prince Ali
lines.text: That is very very kind of you, how do I get out?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: With a disguise. I have removed the Lady Keli. She is tied up, but will not stay tied up for long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take this disguise, and this key.

lines.speaker: Prince Ali
lines.text: Thank you my friend, I must leave you now. My father will pay you well for this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go to Leela, she is close to here.

sections.title: Talking to Lady Keli after she reappears

lines.speaker: Lady Keli
lines.text: You tricked me, and tied me up, Guards kill this stranger!!

lines.speaker: Jail Guard
lines.text: Yes M'lady

sections.title: Talking to Joe after rescuing the Prince

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: Halt! Who goes there? Friend or foe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi friend, I am just checking out things here.

lines.speaker: Joe
lines.text: The Prince got away, I am in trouble. I better not talk to you, they are not sure I was drunk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't say anything, your secret is safe with me.

lines.speaker: Leela
lines.text: Thank you, Al-Kharid will forever owe you for your help. I think that if there is ever anything that needs to be done, you will be someone they can rely on.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: The prince is safe and on his way home with Leela. You can pick up your payment from the chancellor.

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.text: You have the eternal gratitude of the Emir for rescuing his son. I am authorised to pay you 700 coins.
